zh.lifeSummary: 當Raymond II des Baux - d'Orange於1188年出生在法國布-度-o萊斯 包-德-普洛福斯時，他的父親Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orange25歲，母親Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabran16歲。他和Alasacie de Marseille至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1236年6月23日在他的家鄉享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Raymond II des Baux - d'Orange于1188年出生在法国布-度-o萊斯 包-德-普洛福斯时，他的父亲Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orange25岁，母亲Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabran16岁。他和Alasacie de Marseille至少有4个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1236年6月23日在他的家乡享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: des
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German (Düs):
topographic name from Westphalian dialect dūs ‘pile, decay’.
variant of   Duis  .

variants.name: Duis
meanings: North German and Dutch: from a personal name derived from the name Theodorus (see Theodore ) or from a personal name based on the ancient Germanic element theud ‘people, race’.

nameMeanings.name: Baux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin; mainly Leicestershire): nickname from Old French, Middle English baud ‘cheerful, sprightly’.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, based on the element bald ‘bold, strong’.

variants.name: Baudet
meanings: French: from a pet form of an ancient Germanic personal name based on the element bald ‘bold, strong’ (see Baud ).

variants.name: Beaudin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Urbain, Alcid, Andre, Briand, Donat, Fernand, Gilles, Marcel, Renald, Stephane.
French: from a pet form of an ancient Germanic personal name based on the element bald ‘bold, strong’ (see Baud ). Compare Baudin 1.
Altered form of French   Bodin  . Compare   Baudin   2,   Boda   5, and   Bodi   6.

variants.name: Baudin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Clothilde, Jacques, Jean-Paul, Pierre.
French: from a pet form of an ancient Germanic personal name based on the element bald ‘bold, strong’ (see Baud ).
Altered form of French Canadian   Beaudin   2.

nameMeanings.name: d'Orange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from one of the many places called Orange in France, perhaps most prominently Orange in Vaucluse, from which the noble House of Orange took its name. It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin.

English: usually a variant of Orridge, either from the Middle English personal name Ordrich, Orrich (Old English Ordrīc, from ord ‘point, spear’ + rīc ‘power, realm’), with post-medieval voicing of the final consonant to give Orridge, or from a de-aspirated form of Horridge and Horwich (both habitational names from any of the places called Horwich in Lancashire and Derbyshire) with the addition of an intrusive -n-.

English: from the Middle English (Old French) female personal name Orenge, probably a variant of Orege (from Latin Oregia) with Anglo-Norman French intrusive -n- before -g-, as in English passenger for Old French passager.

variants.name: Orris
meanings: English (East Anglia): probably in most instances a variant of Orridge (see Orange 2 and 3), with sibilation of the final palatal consonant to /ʃ/ and /s/ to give (H)orrish and (H)orris. It was sometimes associated with the personal name Horace in modern times by folk etymology.

English (Essex): occasionally perhaps from the Middle English personal name Orace (from Latin Horatius, possibly of Etruscan origin) but it was exceptionally rare in medieval England and is unlikely to be the main source of the surname.

variants.name: Horwich
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Horwich (Lancashire), probably from Old English hār ‘gray, stone’, also ‘boundary’, + wice ‘wych elm’. See also Horace .

nameMeanings.name: Raymond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Raimund, of Germanic origin, from ragin ‘advice, decision’ + mund ‘protector’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. Subsequently it dropped out of use, but was revived in the middle of the 19th century, together with several other given names of Old English and Norman origin.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Loving’.

en.lifeSummary: When Raymond II des Baux - d'Orange was born in 1188, in Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, France, his father, Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orange, was 25 and his mother, Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabran, was 16. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Alasacie de Marseille. He died on 23 June 1236, in his hometown, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Raymond II des Baux - d'Orange est né en 1188, à Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, France, son père, Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orange, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabran, avait 16 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Alasacie de Marseille. Il est décédé le 23 juin 1236, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Raymond II des Baux - d'Orange 1188 in Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orange 25 und seine Mutter Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabran 16. Er hatte mit Alasacie de Marseille mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 23. Juni 1236 im Alter von 48 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Raymond II des Baux - d'Orange è nato il nel 1188 nel Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, Francia, il suo padre, Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orange, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabran, aveva 16 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Alasacie de Marseille. Morì il 23 giugno 1236, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Raymond II des Baux - d'Orangeが1188年にフランスボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンレス バウクス-デ-プローベンスで生まれた時，父Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orangeは25歳，母Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabranは16歳であった。彼はAlasacie de Marseilleとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1236年6月23日に故郷で48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Raymond II des Baux - d'Orange(이)가 1188년 프랑스 부체스-드-ho니 레스 바스-데-프로븐스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orange(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabran(은)는 16세였다. 그는 Alasacie de Marseille(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1236년 6월 23일 그의 고향에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Raymond II des Baux - d'Orange nasceu em 1188, em Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, França, seu pai, Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orange, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabran, tinha 16 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Alasacie de Marseille. Ele faleceu em 23 de junho de 1236, em sua cidade natal, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Raymond II des Baux - d'Orange родился в 1188 г. в местности лесь баукс-де-провенс, боучес-ду-рoн, Франция, его отцу по имени Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orange было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabran, 16. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Alasacie de Marseille. Он умер 23 июня 1236 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Raymond II des Baux - d'Orange nació en 1188, en Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, Francia, su papá, Bertrand II des Baux-d'Orange, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Ermengarz de Mévouillon de Sabran, tenía 16 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Alasacie de Marseille. Murió el 23 de junio de 1236, en su pueblo, a la edad de 48 años.
